etymology.description: 同(同) (tóng, "together") + 事(事) (shì, "affair; matter") = people doing affairs together -> colleague
etymology.interpretation: 同(同) (tóng, "together") represents the idea of people or actions being united in purpose or place. 事(事) (shì, "affair; matter") refers to tasks or duties one engages in. Thus, 同事 (tóngshì) literally means 'together in affairs' or 'sharing a common task,' which conceptually leads to 'colleague,' as colleagues are those who work together on shared tasks and responsibilities within a workplace.

examples.chinese: 我和同事一起吃午饭(我和同事一起吃午飯)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒ hé tóngshì yīqǐ chī wǔfàn.
examples.english: I have lunch with my colleague.

examples.chinese: 昨天晚上，我和同事加班到很晚(昨天晚上，我和同事加班到很晚)。
examples.pinyin: Zuótiān wǎnshàng, wǒ hé tóngshì jiābān dào hěn wǎn.
examples.english: Last night, I and my colleague worked overtime until late.

examples.chinese: 她的新同事很友好(她的新同事很友好)。
examples.pinyin: Tā de xīn tóngshì hěn yǒuhǎo.
examples.english: Her new colleague is very friendly.
